channel.title: The Athletic - Pittsburgh Steelers (NFL)
channel.description: Pittsburgh Steelers news from The Athletic (NYTimes).

item.title: Submit your questions for Mike DeFabo’s Steelers mailbag
item.description: Summer is almost here, and Mike McCarthy's first Steelers team continues to take shape.

item.title: Patrick Mahomes is again the league's highest-paid quarterback
item.description: Patrick Mahomes' latest record-setting deal, plus what we've learned from OTAs so far, including buzz on Aaron Donald, A.J. Brown and Lions

item.title: Can Q-Tip and OutKast help DC Patrick Graham bring back the Steel Curtain?
item.description: It's the first step in Graham's plan to restore the Steelers as one of the NFL's elite defenses.

item.title: Broderick Jones might be a bust, but Omar Khan's first Steelers draft class is thriving
item.description: A look back at the tremendous depth Pittsburgh added in a pivotal 2023 draft.

item.title: What's it like to be a Cowboys fan? Plus: Why Russell Wilson is not a Hall of Fame quarterback
item.description: Cheering for Dallas is like ... as explained by fans, plus why the Seahawks Super Bowl-winning quarterback belongs in the Hall of Very Good

item.title: Is Russell Wilson a Hall of Famer? QB has a path to Canton, but it's complicated
item.description: The end of Wilson's career was mostly a disaster, but that doesn't nullify how truly elite he was during his first decade in the NFL.

item.title: T.J. Watt on the move, safety still a need: 10 takeaways from Steelers OTAs
item.description: Plus, two mid-round Steelers rookies stand out, and a former first-rounder tries to make it as a tryout player.

item.title: Steelers, TE Darnell Washington agree to 4-year, $42 million extension: Source
item.description: It wasn’t until Aaron Rodgers arrived last summer that Washington became more of a threat in the passing game.

item.title: Russell Wilson announces he is retiring, joining CBS Sports
item.description: Wilson is pivoting after completing his 14th NFL season last year with the New York Giants.

item.title: Steelers gave Nick Herbig a $100 million contract. What will Joey Porter Jr. get?
item.description: As with Herbig, the star cornerback was holding in to start mandatory minicamp.

item.title: Pittsburgh Steelers, Nick Herbig agree to 4-year, $100 million deal: Sources
item.description: The linebacker said earlier on Tuesday that he wanted to be "a Steeler for life."

item.title: What to watch at NFL minicamps this week: Steelers, Dolphins first to get underway
item.description: Mike McCarthy will hold his first mandatory sessions with his hometown Steelers, while a new regime in Miami also gathers.

item.title: Max Iheanachor's NFL journey started with a search for size 17 cleats, PB&J quota
item.description: The Steelers' first-round pick, who never played high school football, took a leap of faith — more than once — on his journey to the NFL.

item.title: Steelers part ways with assistant coach Derius Swinton II for violation of team policy: Source
item.description: The Steelers hired Swinton as senior assistant special teams coach in February.

item.title: Steelers rebuilt their WR depth chart, but they still need Roman Wilson to deliver
item.description: The third-year receiver has a chance to carve out a significant role in a deep group of wide reeceivers.

item.title: New O-line coach James Campen could be the key to unlocking the Steelers' future
item.description: The veteran coach has a long track record of developing talent, and few teams have invested in young O-linemen like the Steelers.

item.title: My favorite offseason move by every NFL team: Bengals swing big, Chiefs add juice
item.description: The Bengals, Broncos and Rams made win-now trades, and the Chiefs added a much-needed boost to their run game.

item.title: Early OTA takeaways for Browns, Giants, Falcons and others with new head coaches
item.description: Early insights from OTAs in Cleveland, New York and elsewhere, plus a reader question on mobile quarterbacks

item.title: Steelers have a QB competition — one with immediate and long-term consequences
item.description: Mike McCarthy and the Steelers have decisions to make behind Aaron Rodgers on the depth chart.

item.title: Writing off Aaron Rodgers misses the point. Why not just enjoy his final ride?
item.description: However Rodgers' final act ends, this will be a quest worth watching — one with a tinge of redemption and defiance.

item.title: How Aaron Rodgers helped bring Mike McCarthy to Pittsburgh — and vice versa
item.description: On Wednesday, the quarterback revealed that he'd talked to the Steelers about McCarthy during the hiring process.

item.title: Aaron Rodgers says 2026 will be his final NFL season
item.description: Rodgers agreed to a one-year deal with the Steelers last weekend.

item.title: Steelers DC Patrick Graham has the NFL’s highest-paid defense, a blessing and a burden
item.description: The first-year defensive coordinator knows all about the expectations in Pittsburgh.

item.title: Why Aaron Rodgers showing up to OTAs matters more than ever: Steelers notebook
item.description: Plus, the QB2 battle is underway, and the first look at the offensive line at the first practice of OTAs.

item.title: Pittsburgh reunites Aaron Rodgers & Mike McCarthy and all I can think is ... meh
item.description: Aaron Rodgers is officially back in Pittsburgh. Plus, this season's best games to watch, Robert Mays explaining the Vikings' savviness.
